title: <<COMPONENTNAME>> southbound adapter configuration
description: The configuration <<COMPONENTNAME>> itself understands: the object at `component.global`, with each entry of `component.instances[]` validated against `#/$defs/device`. A southbound adapter models ONE DEVICE PER INSTANCE — how to reach it, how often to read it, and what it is permitted to write. See docs/SOUTHBOUND.md for the contract this implements. The library envelope (logging, messaging, metricEmission, heartbeat, streaming, credentials, parameters, health, hierarchy, identity, topic, tags) is owned by the canonical schema (schema/edgecommons-config-schema.json) and is deliberately NOT redeclared here. This schema is derived from the scaffold's example config (test-configs/<<COMPONENTNAME>>.json) and the keys the <<SNAKENAME>> package reads.

defaults.description: Defaults applied to every device that does not override them.

pollIntervalMs.description: Milliseconds between reads of each device in the adapter's poll loop. Subscribe-based protocols ignore it.

staleSignalSecs.description: A signal with no update for longer than this counts toward `southbound_health.staleSignals` (SOUTHBOUND.md §5). A signal that silently stops updating is otherwise indistinguishable from one that is simply not changing — which is why staleness is measured rather than assumed.

device.title: One device
device.description: One entry of `component.instances[]`: a single field device / endpoint. Each device runs on its own worker thread and publishes to its own UNS data topics (ecv1/{device}/{component}/{instance}/data/{signalPath}).

id.description: Unique device id. It is the `{instance}` token of this device's UNS topics and the `instance` dimension of its health metric, so it must be a valid UNS token (lower-kebab) and it must be stable.

adapter.description: Which protocol backend to use. Matches `DeviceBackend.kind()` in the <<SNAKENAME>>/device.py seam, and is published in the `device.adapter` field of every SouthboundSignalUpdate (for example `opcua`, `modbus`, `ethernetip`).

connection.description: How to reach the device. Deliberately OPEN — every protocol needs different keys (a unit id, a security policy, a slave address, a serial port + baud rate). This is the one place the adapter should not be strict; everything else stays `additionalProperties: false`.

endpoint.description: The endpoint, in whatever form the protocol uses. Published in the `device.endpoint` field of every SouthboundSignalUpdate.

pollIntervalMs.description: Per-device override of the read cadence, in milliseconds. Falls back to `defaults.pollIntervalMs` (or 5000).

allow.description: Signal ids this adapter may write, matched on the STABLE `signal.id` (never a volatile index). Anything not listed is refused, whatever an `sb/write` command asks for. An empty list — the default — means the adapter is read-only, which is the correct posture for anything touching a control system.
